zh.lifeSummary: 當William Gerard IV於大約1313年出生在英國爾金斯利時，他的父親Sir William Gerard III11311歲，母親Lady Matilda de Byron22歲。他於大約1334年與Joan de Bryn在英國英格蘭爾金斯利結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。他去世於1376年之後在英國藍卡爾布萊恩。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当William Gerard IV于大约1313年出生在英国爾金斯利时，他的父亲Sir William Gerard III11311岁，母亲Lady Matilda de Byron22岁。他于大约1334年与Joan de Bryn在英国英格兰爾金斯利结婚。他们至少是2个儿子的父母。他去世于1376年之后在英国藍卡爾布萊恩。

nameMeanings.name: Gerard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Camille, Jean-Pierre, Murielle, Emile, Pascal, Pierre, Agathe, Alain, Aldes, Antoine, Armand.

French (Gérard), Walloon (mainly Gérard), and English (of Norman origin): from the personal name Gérard, Gerard (from Old French Gerart, Girart), composed of the ancient Germanic elements gēr ‘spear, lance’ + hard ‘hardy, brave, strong’. As a surname of French origin it is also found in Haiti. Compare Garrard and also French Guerard , English Garard , Gerrard , and Jarrard .

variants.name: Gerardi
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Vito, Enzo, Gaspare, Luigi, Rocco, Antonio, Canio, Carmine, Filippo, Francesco, Giovanni, Libero.
Italian: patronymic or plural form of the ancient Germanic personal name Gerardo (see Gerard ). This surname is also found in southern France.
German and French (mainly Nord): Latinized (humanistic) patronymic from the personal name German Gerhard , French Gérard (see Gerard ), using the genitive of the Latinized form Gerardus.

variants.name: Garrison
meanings: English: from a shortened form of Middle English Gerardson, a patronymic from the personal name Gerard + son, or an Anglicized form of its Dutch cognate Gerritsen . The English surname is probably in most cases of Flemish or Dutch origin. It was brought to Britain by immigrants from the Low Countries, especially in the 15th and 16th centuries.
Americanized form of Jewish   Gerson   (see   Gershon  ).
Americanized form of Dutch   Gerritsen  .

variants.name: Garrard
meanings: English (London and East Anglia): variant of   Gerard  .
Altered form of French Gérard (see Gerard ).
History: This surname (see 2 above) is listed in the register of Huguenot ancestors recognized by the Huguenot Society of South Carolina.

variants.name: Geenen
meanings: Dutch, Flemish, and North German: patronymic from the personal name Geen, a pet form of Gerard .

variants.name: Gerardo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Francisco, Alberto, Alfredo, Aurelio, Carlos, Jesus, Juan, Norberto, Ramon, Tomas, Ana.  Italian Antonio, Carmine, Dino, Enrico, Marco, Marco Antonio.
Spanish and Italian: from the personal name Gerardo (see Gerard ).

variants.name: Gerald
meanings: English (of Norman origin); French (Gérald): from the ancient Germanic personal name Gerald, Girald, composed of the elements gēr ‘spear, lance’ and wald ‘power, authority’. It was introduced to Britain by the Normans and was fairly common, but less so than Gerard , with which it was early confused. In France the much more common variants of this surname are Géraud and Giraud .

variants.name: Gerrard
meanings: English: variant of   Gerard  .

variants.name: Geron
meanings: French (Géron): from the personal name Gero, Gerone, a short form of ancient Germanic names formed with the element gār, gēr ‘spear, lance’, as for example Gérard (see Gerard ).
Altered form of French Guérin (see Guerin ).
English (Lancashire): of Norman origin, from the personal name Geron of ancient Germanic origin, a cognate of 1 above.

variants.name: Gherardi
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Silvio, Alessandro, Angelo, Antonio, Donato, Egidio, Ennio, Enzo, Gildo, Lucio, Luigi, Primo.
Italian: patronymic or plural form of Gherardo, one of themany Italian equivalents of Gerard .

variants.name: Garard
meanings: English (London and Buckinghamshire): variant of   Gerard  .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When William Gerard IV was born about 1313, in Kingsley, Cheshire, England, his father, Sir William Gerard III, was 11311 and his mother, Lady Matilda de Byron, was 22. He married Joan de Bryn about 1334, in Kingsley, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. He died after 1376, in Bryn, Lancashire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William Gerard IV est né environ 1313, à Kingsley, Cheshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir William Gerard III, avait 11311 ans et sa mère, Lady Matilda de Byron, avait 22 ans. Il a épousé Joan de Bryn environ 1334, à Kingsley, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Il est décédé après 1376, à Bryn, Lancashire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als William Gerard IV um 1313 in Kingsley, Cheshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir William Gerard III 11311 und seine Mutter Lady Matilda de Byron 22. Er hat um 1334 in Kingsley, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom Joan de Bryn geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne. Er starb nach 1376 in Bryn, Lancashire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando William Gerard IV è nato il circa 1313 nel Kingsley, Cheshire, England, il suo padre, Sir William Gerard III, aveva 11311 anni e il suo madre, Lady Matilda de Byron, aveva 22 anni. Lui sposato Joan de Bryn circa 1334, nel Kingsley, Cheshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli. Morì dopo il 1376, nel Bryn, Lancashire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: William Gerard IVが約1313年にイギリスチェシュアイアキングスリーで生まれた時，父Sir William Gerard IIIは11311歳，母Lady Matilda de Byronは22歳であった。約1334年にイギリスイギリスチェシュアイアキングスリーでJoan de Brynと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子の親であった。彼は1376年以後にイギリスランカシュアイアブリンで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: William Gerard IV(이)가 대략 1313년 영국 체실르 킨그슬리에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir William Gerard III(은)는 11311세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Matilda de Byron(은)는 22세였다. 그는 대략 1334년 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 킨그슬리에서 Joan de Bryn(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1376년 이후 영국 랭카실르 브린에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando William Gerard IV nasceu aproximadamente 1313, em Kingsley, Cheshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir William Gerard III, tinha 11311 anos e sua mãe, Lady Matilda de Byron, tinha 22 anos. Ele casou-se com Joan de Bryn aproximadamente 1334, em Kingsley, Cheshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos. Ele faleceu após 1376, em Bryn, Lancashire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William Gerard IV родился около 1313 г. в местности кингсли, чешир, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir William Gerard III было 11311 год, а матери, которую звали Lady Matilda de Byron, 22. Он женился на женщине по имени Joan de Bryn около 1334 г. в местности кингсли, чешир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Он умер после 1376 г. в местности брин, ланкашир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando William Gerard IV nació aproximadamente 1313, en Kingsley, Cheshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir William Gerard III, tenía 11311 años y su mamá, Lady Matilda de Byron, tenía 22 años. Se casó con Joan de Bryn aproximadamente 1334, en Kingsley, Cheshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos. Murió después de 1376, en Bryn, Lancashire, Inglaterra.
